text: Clone your voice for every demo. Record fifteen seconds of natural speech, and Argo turns it into narration that sounds like you. All local, all private — no data leaves your machine.
The workflow is three steps. Record a reference clip. Preview the cloned output. Then run the full pipeline. Each step runs entirely on your machine.
Four reasons to clone. Total privacy — everything stays local. Fast iteration with the preview script. High fidelity from Qwen3 tee tee ess. And zero rewrite — same manifests you already use.

Step one: record your reference. Run the recording script, read a phoneme-rich sentence, and the tool captures fifteen seconds of clean audio. It applies noise reduction and normalization automatically.

overlay.text: One script captures and normalizes your reference voice

text: Step two: preview. The voice clone preview script sends your voiceover manifest to the M.L.X. audio server. It generates each clip individually so you can listen, tweak, and iterate before committing.

overlay.text: Preview each cloned clip before the full pipeline run

text: Step three: configure. Add M.L.X. audio as your tee tee ess engine, point it at your reference clip, and you're done. One config change, and every argo pipeline run uses your cloned voice.

overlay.text: Point your config at mlx-audio with refAudio — done

text: Three models to choose from. Spark for speed. Qwen3 for the best clone quality — it captures your tone, pacing, and vocal character. And C.S.M. for conversational style.

overlay.text: Qwen3-TTS delivers the best clone quality

text: The argo preview command lets you open a browser-based viewer after your first pipeline run. Edit voiceover text, adjust overlays, and regenerate tee tee ess per scene — without re-recording.

overlay.title: argo preview
overlay.body: Edit voiceover, overlays, and timing in your browser.

text: From a fifteen second voice clip to a polished demo video. Your voice, your brand, powered by Argo.

overlay.text: Your voice. Your demos. Powered by Argo.
